meta.title: MailChamp — Enterprise AI Email Compose
meta.description: Gmail-quality AI email compose with advanced prompting, insights, and a polished mail archive UX.

landing.headline: MailChamp
landing.subhead: Turn intent, facts, and tone into boardroom-ready drafts — Gmail-quality polish, research-backed prompts, zero inbox fiction.
landing.story1Body: Advanced chain-of-thought prompting outperforms naive baselines in our Python eval — advanced wins at 0.878.
landing.story2Body: All Mail, Drafts, Sent, and Archive. Three-pane desktop shell. Keyboard-first. No SMTP theater.
landing.story3Body: Keys never touch the browser. Next proxies short-lived JWTs to our Go API on Render.
landing.featureAiDesc: Intent, key facts, tone, and strategy — TipTap drafts in seconds.
landing.featureMailDesc: Dense list, reading pane, archive, hard delete — built for speed.
landing.featureInsightsDesc: Recharts from our real Python evaluation report — not vanity metrics.

auth.name: Name
auth.noForgot: Remember your password — reset email is not available in v1.
auth.toLogin: Already have an account? Sign in

blog.title: Blog
blog.subtitle: Notes on prompting, eval, and product craft.

docs.title: Docs
docs.subtitle: Get productive with MailChamp fast.
docs.gettingStartedBody: Sign up, open Compose, enter intent + facts + tone, Generate, then Save Draft or Mark Sent.
docs.foldersBody: All Mail shows drafts and sent. Drafts, Sent, and Archive map 1:1 to status. Delete is permanent.
docs.apiBody: Gemini runs only in Go. The browser talks to Next; Next mints a short JWT for the API. Cold starts can take up to ~90s on free tiers.
docs.promptsBody: Edit root prompts/, sync to api/prompts/, redeploy the API.

pricing.title: Pricing
pricing.subtitle: Built on a true $0 stack. No cards. No surprises.
pricing.freeDesc: Full compose, mail archive, and Insights for personal / portfolio demos.
pricing.teamDesc: Shared workspaces and SSO are not shipping yet — this card is enterprise chrome only.
pricing.note: Vercel Hobby is non-commercial. If we monetize later, we leave Hobby — that's policy, not a billing trap today.

about.title: About
about.body1: MailChamp started as a research eval CLI comparing advanced vs naive email prompting strategies.
about.body2: We kept the Python pipeline as the source of truth for Insights, then wrapped a Gmail-quality web UX around the winning approach.
about.body3: Stack philosophy: own our Postgres, keep secrets server-side, stay $0-maintainable, ship enterprise polish without enterprise invoices.

contact.title: Contact
contact.subtitle: Say hello — no SMTP backend in v1; we open your mail client with a prefilled message.
contact.name: Name
contact.hint: Prefer GitHub issues on the project repo once published.
